topic: Structural Concepts / Soils and Retaining Walls
question: Which of the following soils has the smallest angle of repose and produces the largest lateral force against a retaining wall?

choices.text: Gravel
Hardpan
Saturated silt
Dense, moist sand

answer: C. Saturated silt
keyMemory: Saturated silt
explanation:
Question 1
Basahin muna natin ang full question:
“Which of the following soils has the smallest angle of repose and produces the largest lateral force against a retaining wall?”

Choices:
A. Gravel
B. Hardpan
C. Saturated silt
D. Dense, moist sand

Correct answer: C. Saturated silt.
Uulitin natin: ang tamang sagot ay Saturated silt.

Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang clue sa tanong ay “soils, smallest, angle, repose, produces.” Sa Structural Concepts, lagi mong iisipin ang load path, support condition, material behavior, at failure mode. Dito, ang pinaka-exact na sagot sa wording ng item ay Saturated silt.

B. Hardpan — related-sounding siya, pero hindi ito ang exact structural term or behavior na hinihingi. Tanggalin ito kung hindi tugma sa load path, failure mode, member type, or standard definition.
C. Saturated silt — ito ang tama. Ito ang pinaka-direct match sa clue at structural concept ng item.

Step 3: Tanggalin ang choices na descriptive lang pero hindi standard structural term.
Step 4: Tanggalin ang choices na ibang behavior: halimbawa bending vs torsion, axial vs shear, dead load vs live load, buckling vs crushing.
Step 5: Piliin ang term na pinaka-direct sa definition: Saturated silt.

Keyword at trap word:

question: Which of the following terms best describes the property that causes a freely vibrating structure to come to rest eventually?

Vibration

“Which of the following terms best describes the property that causes a freely vibrating structure to come to rest eventually?”

B. Damping

question: According to the Structural Concepts additional notes, which statement best matches “Coarse Aggregate”?

choices.text: Nominal maximum coarse aggregate size: 1/5 narrowest form dimension, 1/3 slab depth, and 3/4 minimum clear rebar spacing.
Maximum distance from bolt/rivet center to nearest edge must not exceed 12t and must not exceed 150 mm.
Windward side = side facing the incoming wind and usually experiencing positive pressure.
Camber = intentional upward curvature built into a beam/girder/truss to offset expected downward deflection.

answer: A. Nominal maximum coarse aggregate size: 1/5 narrowest form dimension, 1/3 slab depth, and 3/4 minimum clear rebar spacing.
keyMemory: Nominal maximum coarse aggregate size: 1/5 narrowest form dimension, 1/3 slab depth, and 3/4 minimum clear rebar spacing.
explanation:

Bakit ito ang tama?
Ito ang exact board-memory point mula sa Additional Notes Page 1. Notes-to-cards conversion ito, kaya ang hinahanap ay tamang rule, value, definition, or behavior, hindi computation-heavy answer.

Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
Yung ibang choices ay structural-related din, pero para sa ibang topic. Sa ganitong reviewer notes, ang key skill ay i-match ang exact heading sa exact rule. Kung “Coarse Aggregate” ang tanong, ang sagot dapat tungkol mismo sa Concrete Technology, hindi tungkol sa ibang steel, concrete, seismic, wind, or beam concept.

Elimination method:
Step 1: Basahin ang heading: “Coarse Aggregate.”

Step 3: Tanggalin ang choices na tama sa ibang heading pero hindi dito.
Step 4: Piliin ang exact memory statement: Nominal maximum coarse aggregate size: 1/5 narrowest form dimension, 1/3 slab depth, and 3/4 minimum clear rebar spacing..

Keyword at trap word:
Ang keyword dito ay “Coarse Aggregate.” Ang trap ay mga numbers/terms na tama elsewhere pero hindi tugma sa heading.

sourceOriginalId: Additional Notes Page 14: Fracture
question: According to the Structural Concepts additional notes, which statement best matches “Fracture”?

choices.text: Fracture is the actual breaking or rupture of material after ultimate and post-ultimate deformation.
Preferred slenderness limit: compression members 200; tension members 300.

Stirrup = closed or U-shaped reinforcement used to resist shear and hold longitudinal bars.
